capabilities.title: Monorepo scaffolding (agent/hub/dashboard/shared)
capabilities.description: pnpm workspace with four packages: agent (PTY/container daemon), hub (Fastify orchestrator), dashboard (Nuxt SPA), shared (types/protocol). Turborepo for build caching. Package boundaries enforced via lint rules.
capabilities.user_value: Foundation layer that lets every downstream capability exist in the correct package with typed cross-package contracts.
acceptance_criteria: Package boundary rules enforce shared-only imports

capabilities.title: Shared types package with Zod schemas
capabilities.description: packages/shared exports types, Zod schemas, and WebSocket protocol definitions consumed by agent, hub, and dashboard. Single source of truth for protocol contracts.
capabilities.user_value: Eliminates protocol drift between processes; catches schema mismatches at compile time.
acceptance_criteria: shared/src/protocol.ts exports Zod schemas for all WS messages
Zod .parse() runs on every received message

capabilities.title: Node 20+ and Fastify 5.x / @fastify/websocket 11.x version lock
capabilities.description: Root package.json specifies engines.node >=20 (required by better-sqlite3 v12+). Hub package locks to fastify ^5.x and @fastify/websocket ^11.x (raw WebSocket API, not stream).
capabilities.user_value: Prevents subtle runtime errors from version mismatch; team and CI know exact minimum runtime.
acceptance_criteria: WebSocket handlers use raw WebSocket API

capabilities.title: Hub core server (Fastify + SQLite WAL + health check)
capabilities.description: Fastify server with pino logger, better-sqlite3 connection in WAL mode, health check endpoint, migration runner. PRAGMAs: journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL, cache_size=-20000, mmap_size=268435456, busy_timeout=5000, foreign_keys=ON.
capabilities.user_value: Reliable, durable backend with safe concurrent reads and bounded memory; enables downstream features to persist and serve data.
acceptance_criteria: Hub boots and listens on configured port
SQLite opens with WAL PRAGMAs applied
Migration runner applies migrations idempotently on startup

capabilities.title: Agent daemon core (PTY pool + WS client + session lifecycle)
capabilities.description: Agent daemon runs on machines, maintains node-pty pool for sessions, reports session lifecycle events to Hub over WebSocket. Handles SessionStart, SessionEnd, Notification, SubagentStart, PreCompact, PostCompact (events that lack HTTP hook support).
capabilities.user_value: Gives Hub real-time visibility into sessions running on remote machines without requiring per-session HTTP plumbing.
acceptance_criteria: Agent maintains WS connection to Hub with heartbeat + reconnect
PTY sessions can be started, attached, detached, killed
Lifecycle events are sent as typed protocol messages

capabilities.title: Binary WebSocket terminal I/O with buffered resume
capabilities.description: Terminal I/O uses binary WS frames with a single-byte channel prefix (0=STDIN, 1=STDOUT, 2=STDERR, 3=error) instead of JSON-wrapped messages. Hub maintains a circular buffer of recent output per session so new viewers and reconnecting clients resume without loss.
capabilities.user_value: Efficient multi-viewer terminal access with seamless reconnection, matching ttyd/Kubernetes patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Terminal frames use channel prefix byte
Hub buffers last 30min of output per session
New viewers receive buffered output on connect
Reconnecting clients resume from last position

capabilities.title: Subscribe/unsubscribe WebSocket semantics
capabilities.description: Dashboard clients send explicit subscribe/unsubscribe messages per resource (session, machine, queue) with optional id. Hub only sends updates for subscribed resources. Follows GitLab Watch Manager pattern.
capabilities.user_value: Dashboard scales to hundreds of sessions without flooding each client with irrelevant updates.
acceptance_criteria: Hub only pushes updates for subscribed ids
Wildcard subscription (no id) subscribes to all of that resource

capabilities.title: session:event WebSocket message type for hook events
capabilities.description: New protocol message { type:'session:event', sessionId, event:{ type, payload, receivedAt } } pushes hook events (tool use, sub-agent, permission request, completion) from Hub to subscribed dashboard clients in real time.
capabilities.user_value: Dashboard Events tab reflects live agent activity without polling.
acceptance_criteria: Hub emits session:event on hook receipt
Event persisted so Events tab can rebuild history

capabilities.title: Session restart, clone, and bulk action endpoints
capabilities.description: REST endpoints POST /api/sessions/:id/restart, /clone, and bulk variants POST /api/sessions/bulk/kill, /bulk/clone accepting { sessionIds[], targetMachineId? }. Enables fast re-run and parallel sweeps.
capabilities.user_value: Users can re-run failed sessions or fan out variations without manual re-entry.
acceptance_criteria: Restart kills existing and queues new with same spec
Clone queues new with identical spec and optional machine override
Bulk endpoints accept id arrays and return task IDs

capabilities.title: Session tags column and tag-based filtering
capabilities.description: Add tags TEXT (JSON array) to sessions and queue. API accepts tags on create. Dashboard session list has tag filter pill selector and tag suggestions.
capabilities.user_value: Operators organize sessions by project/type/custom dimension; filter views reduce cognitive load.
acceptance_criteria: List view has tag filter and search

capabilities.title: Session timeout and max cost enforcement
capabilities.description: Add timeout_seconds INTEGER and max_cost_usd REAL to sessions/queue. Hub enforces by killing sessions exceeding either limit, marking status failed with reason 'timeout' or 'cost_limit_exceeded'. Checks run on a 5-10s interval.
capabilities.user_value: Prevents runaway costs and stuck sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Enforcement interval kills offenders with correct reason
Dashboard shows remaining budget and time

capabilities.title: Retry policy with backoff for queued tasks
capabilities.description: retry_policy TEXT (JSON: {maxRetries, backoffSeconds, retryOnExitCodes?}) on queue/sessions. On failure Hub re-queues if below maxRetries with exponential backoff. Optional exit-code filter.
capabilities.user_value: Automates recovery from transient failures; raises reliability of long workloads.

capabilities.title: Machine capabilities and task requirements matching
capabilities.description: Machines store capabilities TEXT (JSON array: e.g. 'gpu','repo:my-app','docker:buildx'). Tasks store requirements TEXT. Scheduler filters machines where all task requirements exist in the machine capability set.
capabilities.user_value: Operators pin work to machines with the right resources without manual placement.

capabilities.title: Optional machineId with scoring-based auto-scheduler
capabilities.description: machineId becomes optional. When omitted, Hub runs scheduler: filter machines by requirements, then score = (maxSessions - active)*10 + (100 - cpu%) + (100 - mem%) - (queueDepth*5). Highest score wins, atomic placement.
capabilities.user_value: Fire-and-forget workloads without operator knowing which machine has capacity.
acceptance_criteria: Creating a session without machineId succeeds and queues
Scheduler runs when task moves to running
Fallback behaviour defined when all machines overloaded

capabilities.title: Audit log for mutations
capabilities.description: audit_log table: action, entity_type, entity_id, details JSON, created_at. All session/machine/template/scheduled-task mutations and cost-limit enforcement events logged. GET /api/audit-log with filtering.
capabilities.user_value: Compliance, debugging, and security investigations.
acceptance_criteria: Query endpoint supports filtering by entity/action/time

capabilities.title: Agent SDK session discovery integration
capabilities.description: Hub/agent uses @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk query() and listSessions() for session discovery instead of daemon-only polling.
capabilities.user_value: Reliable session enumeration and state queries without polling.
acceptance_criteria: Type defs match latest SDK release
Discovery endpoint returns SDK-sourced session list

capabilities.title: Dual-stream parsing: stream-json events + PTY ANSI
capabilities.description: Claude Code spawned with --output-format stream-json and PTY attached. stdout bifurcated: JSON-lines to event parser (permissionAsked, toolCalled, costUpdated), ANSI to xterm.js. Rivet-inspired pattern but PTY-aware.
capabilities.user_value: Dashboard gets structured events for logic/routing AND rendered terminal for interaction.
acceptance_criteria: Event parser routes typed events to hub

capabilities.title: Agent writes hook settings.json targeting hub
capabilities.description: Before spawning sessions, agent daemon writes ~/.claude/settings.json configuring PermissionRequest, PreToolUse, and supported HTTP hooks to point at hub's Tailscale URL. Idempotent; preserves user customizations.
capabilities.user_value: Unattended sessions route hook events to hub automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Agent writes settings.json on session setup
Works in PTY and SDK modes

capabilities.title: Hub HTTP hook receivers (PreToolUse, PermissionRequest, TaskCompleted, Stop, etc.)
capabilities.description: POST /hooks/pre-tool-use, /hooks/permission-request, /hooks/task-completed, and the rest of HTTP-capable events. Per-route receives payload, evaluates policy synchronously, persists approval_requests row, returns decision JSON. Only wires events that actually support HTTP hooks (correcting the earlier docs-audit mistake).
capabilities.user_value: Central policy evaluation and audit for every supported hook event.
acceptance_criteria: Routes implemented for every HTTP-capable event
Incorrect HTTP-only events explicitly documented as WS-reported

capabilities.title: Hub SessionStart / SessionEnd / Notification / SubagentStart ingestion via agent WS
capabilities.description: Events that do NOT support HTTP hooks are reported by the agent daemon over the existing WebSocket channel. Hub persists them into the same session event log that HTTP hooks feed.
capabilities.user_value: Unified event stream regardless of whether a given hook is HTTP-capable.
acceptance_criteria: Agent sends command-only hook events on WS
Hub routes them through the same pipeline as HTTP hooks
Session event log is unified across both paths

capabilities.title: approval_requests table and classification schema
capabilities.description: SQLite approval_requests with id, session_id, request_type (permission|ask_user|plan_approval|mcp_elicitation|mcp_auth), tool_name, tool_input JSON, context, status (pending|approved|denied|timed_out|cancelled|error), risk_level, source, timeout_at, resolved_at, resolved_by, rule_id. Indexes on session_id, status, timeout_at.
capabilities.user_value: Persistent audit trail and queryable queue for all approval flows.

capabilities.title: Risk classifier for approval requests
capabilities.description: approvals/risk-classifier.ts assigns low|medium|high|critical based on tool + input pattern. Read/Glob/Grep→low, Write/Edit→medium, Bash→high, dangerous patterns (rm -rf, sudo, curl|bash)→critical, MCP→high.
capabilities.user_value: Enables intelligent routing and timeout escalation based on real risk rather than blanket rules.

capabilities.title: Policy engine with approval_policy_rules table
capabilities.description: approval_policy_rules with priority ordering, matching on tool_name/bash_pattern/path_glob/session_tag/machine_id/risk_level, action enum auto_approve|auto_deny|require_approval. Default-deny for bash, auto-approve read-only ops. Rules creatable via API, Dashboard, or from user 'Approve & Remember'.
capabilities.user_value: Admins standardize auto-approval for safe ops and force review of risky ones centrally.
acceptance_criteria: Default shipped rules load on first boot

capabilities.title: Approval timeout sweeper
capabilities.description: Hub background interval (every 10s) that finds approval_requests with timeout_at<=now and applies timeout_action (default auto_deny, optionally auto_approve for trusted sessions). Defaults: 60s/300s/600s by risk, 30m for ask_user.
capabilities.user_value: Sessions don't hang indefinitely when reviewers are unavailable.
acceptance_criteria: Expired requests resolved with configured action

capabilities.title: SDK canUseTool bridge (long-polling to hub)
capabilities.description: approval-bridge.ts wraps the SDK canUseTool callback: POSTs to hub /api/approvals, long-polls (5-10 min) until decision, retries with backoff on disconnect. Enables SDK/headless sessions (no interactive hooks) to route approvals through the same pipeline.
capabilities.user_value: Queued/background jobs participate in the central approval workflow instead of auto-denying.
acceptance_criteria: canUseTool callback POSTs to hub with tool payload
Policy rules apply the same way as HTTP hook path

capabilities.title: Approval API endpoints (single + bulk CRUD)
capabilities.description: POST /api/approvals/:id/respond, POST /api/approvals/bulk/respond, GET /api/approvals (filter by status, session), GET /api/approvals/:id. Decision enum approve|deny|edit with optional responseText, responseData, rememberAsRule.
capabilities.user_value: Dashboard and external tools can act on approvals, including bulk decisions.

capabilities.title: Three-way approval decision (approve/edit/reject with feedback)
capabilities.description: Detail drawer has three actions: Approve (execute as-is), Edit (modify tool_input then execute), Reject (deny with feedback text that is injected back to Claude as a system message).
capabilities.user_value: Users steer Claude precisely instead of binary approve/deny.
acceptance_criteria: Edit validates edited input against tool schema
Reject feedback appears in session as system message
Edit action logged separately from approve

capabilities.title: Approve-and-Remember rule creation flow
capabilities.description: Checkbox on approve action. If checked, shows rule preview (tool, glob, bash pattern) and saves an approval_policy_rules row with created_from_approval_id. Future matching requests auto-approve.
capabilities.user_value: Policy rules grow organically from real decisions; reduces approval fatigue.
acceptance_criteria: Future matches auto-resolved with rule_id logged

capabilities.title: Typed permission replies (once/always/reject)
capabilities.description: Replace binary approve/deny with three session-scoped replies: once (approve single action), always (remember for remainder of session), reject. Rivet-inspired pattern; session-local only (not persisted across sessions).
capabilities.user_value: Fine-grained control without repetitive approvals.
acceptance_criteria: always cache per (session,tool) honored for rest of session

capabilities.title: AskUserQuestion dialog UI
capabilities.description: Chat-bubble dialog for Claude's AskUserQuestion requests. Renders Claude's question with either multiple-choice buttons or text input; submits response via approval API.
capabilities.user_value: Natural conversation flow instead of formal approval UI for clarifying questions.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple-choice buttons or text field per question type

capabilities.title: MCP elicitation dialog UI (JSON-Schema form)
capabilities.description: Dynamic form rendered from MCP elicitation JSON Schema. Supports text, select, checkbox, date. URL-based auth opens embedded browser or link. Response validated against schema before submission.
capabilities.user_value: Authenticate with MCP servers or provide structured input without terminal forms.

capabilities.title: Notification routing engine (dashboard/browser/Slack/Discord/ntfy.sh)
capabilities.description: Multi-layer notification delivery: dashboard toast (instant, sound for critical), browser Notification API with action buttons, Slack/Discord webhooks with rich blocks, ntfy.sh push. Per-channel templates and per-user preferences.
capabilities.user_value: Users alerted via preferred channel; critical interrupts immediately.
acceptance_criteria: Slack Block Kit uses approve/deny buttons deep-linking to dashboard

capabilities.title: Notification batching and risk-based escalation
capabilities.description: Requests arriving within 5s of each other grouped into one notification. Escalation: critical→urgent immediately, high+age>30s→high, medium+age>60s→normal, low→badge only. Default timeouts per risk align with sweeper.
capabilities.user_value: Avoids notification storms while ensuring critical items always interrupt.

capabilities.title: Dashboard overview page (Z-pattern summary + machines + activity)
capabilities.description: Fleet overview: summary cards (running/queued sessions, online machines, daily cost, tokens), machine cards grid, recent activity feed. Z-pattern layout, live WS updates.
capabilities.user_value: Operator snapshot of fleet health, resource usage, and recent events at a glance.

capabilities.title: Machine cards with health conditions, capacity bar, sparklines
capabilities.description: Machine cards display status dot (Ready/NotReady), warning badges (MemoryPressure/DiskPressure/SessionPressure), session slots progress bar, queue depth, CPU/memory sparklines (last 30min). Heartbeat timeout = 60s triggers NotReady.
capabilities.user_value: Instant assessment of machine health and capacity without drilling in.
acceptance_criteria: Conditions derived from metrics and heartbeat

capabilities.title: Machine context switcher in sidebar
capabilities.description: Cockpit-style dropdown in sidebar listing all machines with status indicator. Selecting scopes session/queue views to that machine without full reload.
capabilities.user_value: Rapid context switching between machines.

capabilities.title: Session list view with inline actions and bulk
capabilities.description: Data table: ID/Name, Status badge, Duration, Machine, Tokens/Cost, Age. Inline icons for Open Terminal/Logs/Kill/Clone. Checkboxes with bulk action bar; 'select all' scoped to current page.
capabilities.user_value: One-click access to any session's terminal/logs/actions without navigation.

capabilities.title: Session status indicator visual system (Carbon pattern)
capabilities.description: Carbon-inspired status pattern: color + icon + text for Queued/Blocked/Running/Waiting for Input/Completed/Failed/Cancelled. WCAG AA contrast; pulsing animation respects prefers-reduced-motion.
capabilities.user_value: Instant state recognition; accessible; consistent across views.
acceptance_criteria: All seven states share a shared component

capabilities.title: Session detail view (tabbed: Overview/Terminal/Logs/Events/Stats)
capabilities.description: Tabbed detail page. Overview shows metadata, Terminal embeds xterm.js with live I/O, Logs supports search + follow, Events renders hook timeline, Stats plots token velocity / cost / context window. Tabs persist scroll across switches.
capabilities.user_value: Complete visibility into what happened in a session.
acceptance_criteria: Events pull from session event log
Stats show live and historical values

capabilities.title: xterm.js v6 scoped package migration
capabilities.description: Replace unscoped xterm / xterm-addon-* imports with @xterm/xterm, @xterm/addon-fit, @xterm/addon-webgl, @xterm/addon-serialize. Canvas addon removed — fallback path is DOM renderer.
capabilities.user_value: Unblocks current xterm.js version and avoids build/runtime failures.

capabilities.title: Approval queue dashboard page (/approvals)
capabilities.description: Approvals table: Risk badge, Session, Tool, Input summary, Age countdown, Approve/Deny actions. Bulk 'Approve All Low-Risk' / 'Deny All' / 'Approve Selected'. Sort by age or risk. Keyboard shortcuts (A=approve, D=deny). Mobile card layout.
capabilities.user_value: Efficient review queue for unattended sessions.
acceptance_criteria: Bulk actions wired to bulk respond endpoint

capabilities.title: In-session approval banner
capabilities.description: Sticky banner on session view listing session's pending approvals with risk badge, tool name, input summary, countdown, quick Approve/Deny, 'Approve All Safe' / 'Deny All'. Collapsible when out of focus.
capabilities.user_value: Handle session-scoped approvals without leaving the terminal view.
acceptance_criteria: Banner shows pending approvals only for current session

capabilities.title: Session templates with variable substitution
capabilities.description: templates table with name, description, icon, prompt (supports {{variables}}), cwd, flags, machine_id, requirements, timeout_seconds, max_cost_usd, variables JSON, tags. New Session modal has template picker gallery; selected template renders variable form; substitution at launch.
capabilities.user_value: Parameterize common workflows (Review PR, Run Tests); share across team.
acceptance_criteria: Gallery + variable form in New Session modal

capabilities.title: Scheduled tasks (cron with K8s concurrency policies)
capabilities.description: scheduled_tasks table with cron_expression, prompt, cwd, flags, machine_id/requirements, timeout/max_cost, concurrency_policy (allow|forbid|replace), enabled, last/next_run_at. Hub evaluates expressions and enqueues sessions at scheduled times.
capabilities.user_value: Time-based automation (backups, reports, maintenance) without external cron.

capabilities.title: Session recording persistence (NDJSON + transcript.jsonl)
capabilities.description: Agent persists full NDJSON session transcript (assistant/user/tool messages) plus terminal frame log. Transcript file written append-only to recordings volume: /var/recordings/<session>.jsonl.
capabilities.user_value: Reviewers and replay depend on a complete transcript.

capabilities.title: Session recording playback with xterm.js serialize addon
capabilities.description: Dashboard replay player embeds xterm.js with serialize addon, streams transcript.jsonl, scrub bar seeks by event timestamp, show message metadata inline with terminal.
capabilities.user_value: Reviewers see exactly what happened in a session without re-running it.

capabilities.title: Recordings volume with retention policy
capabilities.description: Separate named volume recordings:/app/data/recordings. Hub periodically deletes recordings older than RECORDINGS_MAX_AGE_DAYS. Independent backup schedule from database.
capabilities.user_value: Bounded disk usage without manual cleanup.

capabilities.title: Repo registry (repos table + CRUD + auto-detect)
capabilities.description: repos table with url, name, owner, default_branch, auth_method, dependency_manager, node_version, setup_commands, pre/post_flight_commands, env_vars, tags. Endpoints: list/create/get/update, import from GitHub URL with auto-detect, detect (scan lockfile/.nvmrc/devcontainer), sync (git fetch).
capabilities.user_value: Register a repo once and reuse across many jobs.

capabilities.title: Job model and state machine
capabilities.description: jobs table with repo_id, workspace_id, machine_id, title, prompt, branch, pr_number, cost_usd, tokens_used, files_changed, tests_passed, state (pending|provisioning|preparing|running|post_processing|completed|failed|cancelled). sessions.job_id FK. Endpoints create/list/detail/retry/cancel.
capabilities.user_value: Track multi-step work per repo with full lifecycle visibility.

capabilities.title: Workspace manager (provisioning + cleanup)
capabilities.description: workspaces table with status (creating|preparing|ready|active|stale|cleanup|deleted), path, repo_id, branch, is_worktree, disk_usage_bytes, deps_installed_at, expires_at. Agent provisioner handles clone/worktree/setup commands. TTL-based cleanup, max workspaces per machine.
capabilities.user_value: Efficient isolated workspaces per repo with automated cleanup.

capabilities.title: Git worktree lifecycle management
capabilities.description: Agent manages per-job git worktrees: clone repo once per repoId, add worktree at /data/repos/<repoId>/worktrees/<workspaceId> -b chq/<jobId>/<slug> origin/<branch>. Commits and pushes from worktree. Periodic git worktree prune for orphans.
capabilities.user_value: Parallel agents on same repo without lock contention; shared .git reduces disk overhead.

capabilities.title: Environment resolver (package manager + node version detection)
capabilities.description: Agent module detects pnpm/yarn/npm lockfile, python (uv/pip), go mod, cargo. Reads .nvmrc for node version, uses fnm/asdf. Uses @unjs/nypm for detection.
capabilities.user_value: Plug-and-play execution across heterogeneous codebases.

capabilities.title: Pre-flight and post-flight check system
capabilities.description: Repo config stores pre_flight_commands and post_flight_commands. Agent runs pre-flights after setup; failure marks workspace failed and aborts job. Post-flights run after Claude Code exits. Output captured with pass/fail status.
capabilities.user_value: Baseline quality gates (type check, unit tests) validated before human review.
acceptance_criteria: Agent executes commands and streams output
Failure path transitions job to failed

capabilities.title: Secret management via env: and file: references
capabilities.description: Repo config stores secret_env_refs: JSON mapping var names to env:SOURCE or file:/path. Agent resolves at runtime and passes to Claude Code process. Future: vault:, 1password:// scheme. Path traversal guarded.
capabilities.user_value: Secrets stay on agent machines; hub has no sensitive data; auditable access.
acceptance_criteria: Resolution runs in agent, not hub

capabilities.title: Batch job launcher (multi-repo orchestration)
capabilities.description: POST /api/jobs/batch with repoIds or tags filter, shared prompt, branchPrefix, maxConcurrency. Creates job per repo, respects concurrency, supports cancel-all.
capabilities.user_value: Run the same task across many repos with centralized progress tracking.

capabilities.title: WebSocket protocol for workspace and git status
capabilities.description: Add protocol messages: hub:workspace:provision, hub:workspace:cleanup, agent:workspace:{cloning|preparing|ready|error|cleaned}, agent:git:{status|committed|pushed}. Zod-validated.
capabilities.user_value: Real-time workspace lifecycle and git events power dashboard updates and job orchestration.
acceptance_criteria: All message types added to shared protocol
Hub routes and persists each event type

capabilities.title: GitHub App manifest flow wizard
capabilities.description: Dashboard 'Connect to GitHub' button submits manifest JSON to github.com/settings/apps/new. Hub receives callback code, exchanges for app credentials, stores encrypted. Installation callback redirects user to install page.
capabilities.user_value: 3-click GitHub connection instead of 15-step manual setup.
acceptance_criteria: Callback handler exchanges code for credentials
Success page verifies with test API call

capabilities.title: Fine-grained PAT fallback for GitHub
capabilities.description: Alternate setup path for users without public HTTPS: user pastes fine-grained PAT with required scopes (contents, pull_requests, issues, checks, actions, metadata). No webhooks, no Checks API; polling fallback instead.
capabilities.user_value: Single-user dev setups without Tailscale Funnel can still connect GitHub.
acceptance_criteria: Polling mode engaged when PAT used

capabilities.title: Tailscale Funnel webhook endpoint provisioning
capabilities.description: Hub webhook endpoint exposed via Tailscale Funnel as https://<hostname>.<tailnet>.ts.net/webhooks/github. Webhook secret stored and validated on incoming requests.
capabilities.user_value: Public HTTPS URL for GitHub webhooks without ngrok or Cloudflare Tunnel.
acceptance_criteria: Tailscale Funnel configured for Hub webhook port

capabilities.title: GitHub credential storage with token auto-rotation
capabilities.description: Encrypted storage of GitHub App private key. @octokit/auth-app for per-installation Octokit clients. LRU cache (up to 15,000) with 60-min token TTL, refresh at 59-min, JWT iat 30s in past for clock drift.
capabilities.user_value: Protected credentials with automatic token rotation.

capabilities.title: PR auto-creation and lifecycle tracking
capabilities.description: On job completion, agent commits, pushes branch, and calls POST /api/jobs/:id/create-pr. Hub generates PR title + body (prompt, files_changed, tests_passed, cost, duration, recording link) via Octokit. pull_requests table tracks github_pr_number, status, review_status, ci_status.
capabilities.user_value: Job outputs land on GitHub as reviewable PRs with full context automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Webhook updates status as review/CI change

capabilities.title: GitHub Checks API reporting
capabilities.description: Agent creates check run on commit (name: 'Claude HQ Agent'). Updates status as in_progress → completed with conclusion and output (test results, errors).
capabilities.user_value: Reviewers see agent status directly on GitHub PR; integrates with branch protection.

capabilities.title: Workforce dashboard — Repos page
capabilities.description: Card list of registered repos. Each card: name, owner, last_synced_at, active job count, quick actions (Launch Job, Sync, Settings, GitHub link). Filter/search by name or tag.
capabilities.user_value: Fast launch of jobs against any registered repo.

capabilities.title: Workforce dashboard — Job launcher modal
capabilities.description: Modal: repo selector, prompt, branch, machine, advanced (auto-PR, skip pre-flight, max cost, timeout, GitHub issue link). Submit creates job and navigates to detail.
capabilities.user_value: Configured job launch with sensible defaults and advanced escape hatches.

capabilities.title: Workforce dashboard — PR tracker page
capabilities.description: Table of all PRs with repo, title, status/review/CI badges, created_at. Filters by repo/review/CI. Row click → job detail.
capabilities.user_value: Triage agent PRs in one place without switching to GitHub.

capabilities.title: Workforce dashboard — Batch launcher page
capabilities.description: Multi-select repos (or tag filter), shared prompt, concurrency slider (default 3), preview, launch. Batch detail page with per-repo status bars and cancel-batch.
capabilities.user_value: One-click deployment of changes across many repos.
acceptance_criteria: Batch detail with status rows and cancel-all

capabilities.title: Workforce dashboard — Enhanced overview
capabilities.description: Overview cards: Active Jobs, Machines Online, Open PRs, Today's Spend. Activity feed (job started/completed, PR created, test failed, cost alert). Quick actions: Launch Job, Import Repo, View Cost.
capabilities.user_value: Single-pane-of-glass for workforce status.
acceptance_criteria: All cards + activity feed live update

capabilities.title: Session cost capture (SDKResultMessage → session_costs)
capabilities.description: SDK query result captured via SDKResultMessage. session_costs table: session_id, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, thinking_tokens, cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens, cost_usd, duration_ms, num_turns, stop_reason, created_at. Sub-agent and teammate costs roll up.
capabilities.user_value: Accurate per-session cost tracking.

capabilities.title: Pricing table + cost formula
capabilities.description: Current pricing (Opus 4.6 $5/$25, Sonnet 4.6 $3/$15, Haiku 4.5 $1/$5 per MTok). Long-context >200K: 2x input, 1.5x output. Cache write 1.25x, cache read 0.1x. Batch API 50%. Extended thinking billed as output. Formula module with unit tests.
capabilities.user_value: Accurate cost calculation matching actual API charges.

capabilities.title: Per-query budget enforcement (maxBudgetUsd)
capabilities.description: Pass maxBudgetUsd to SDK query; SDK returns SDKResultMessage with subtype 'error_max_budget_usd' on exceed. Hub honors per-session budget based on session type.
capabilities.user_value: Hard limit prevents runaway cost on single queries.
acceptance_criteria: Session marked failed with cost_limit_exceeded reason

capabilities.title: Daily / monthly budget thresholds with notifications
capabilities.description: Hub aggregates daily/monthly costs per user/org. Notifies at 50/75/90/100% of configured budget (idempotent). Optional hard stop at 100% (reject new sessions).
capabilities.user_value: Early warnings before hitting monthly limits.

capabilities.title: Token counting estimator (count_tokens API + @anthropic-ai/tokenizer)
capabilities.description: POST /v1/messages/count_tokens for upfront input token counts (free endpoint). Optional local @anthropic-ai/tokenizer fallback. Cost estimate shown in UI before session starts; actual vs estimate recorded for accuracy validation.
capabilities.user_value: Informed decisions before expensive sessions.

capabilities.title: Cost dashboard page
capabilities.description: Summary cards (today/week/month), daily timeline chart, top-repo pie chart, per-machine table, budget alerts section, CSV export. Time range selector.
capabilities.user_value: Cost visibility and chargeback support.

capabilities.title: OpenTelemetry / Langfuse cost observability
capabilities.description: CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1 + OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT for structured export. Optional Langfuse or Helicone integration for attribution and dashboards.
capabilities.user_value: Detailed observability for optimization and audit.
acceptance_criteria: OTel env vars plumb through to sessions

capabilities.title: Machine metrics collection + time-series storage
capabilities.description: Agent reports CPU%, memory%, disk%, active sessions, queue depth on heartbeat. Hub persists to metrics table (rolling window). Sparklines and health conditions derive from this source.
capabilities.user_value: Backend data source for machine cards, alerts, and scheduler scoring.

capabilities.title: spawnClaudeCodeProcess abstraction (SpawnStrategy interface)
capabilities.description: Single interface SpawnStrategy = ({ command, args, cwd, env, signal }) => SpawnedProcess. Agent selects strategy per machine.execution_backend. Backends: local (default), docker, ssh, wsl2. Uniform stdin/stdout/stderr/signal handling.
capabilities.user_value: Jobs run on any backend without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: All strategies pass same contract test

capabilities.title: Docker exec -i spawn strategy with pre-running container pool
capabilities.description: spawn('docker',['exec','-i','-w',cwd,'-e',env,'claude-worker-N','claude',...]). No -t flag (corrupts JSON-lines). Agent maintains a warm container pool to keep overhead <1s.
capabilities.user_value: Sandboxed execution with sub-second startup.

capabilities.title: SSH spawn strategy for remote execution
capabilities.description: ssh -o BatchMode=yes user@host 'export ENV; cd /path && claude ...'. No -tt flag. AbortSignal kills SSH process.
capabilities.user_value: Distributed workflows on Tailscale-connected machines without Docker.

capabilities.title: WSL2 spawn strategy
capabilities.description: wsl -d <distro> -- <command>. Same SpawnedProcess contract. Specifically for hub running on Windows host with WSL2 agent machines.
capabilities.user_value: First-class support for WSL2 development environments.

capabilities.title: Orchestration Agent container controller (dockerode)
capabilities.description: Agent module using dockerode to create/start/attach/stop/remove containers. Handles stdout/stderr demuxing with container.modem.demuxStream(). Tracks container lifecycle.
capabilities.user_value: Direct Docker control for container execution backend.

capabilities.title: Container security baseline (CAP_DROP + limits + read-only rootfs)
capabilities.description: Every container: CapDrop=ALL, SecurityOpt=no-new-privileges, ReadonlyRootfs=true, tmpfs /tmp (rw,noexec,nosuid, 512MB), Memory=2GB, CpuQuota=1.5 cores, PidsLimit=256, user 1000:1000, network=claude-restricted.
capabilities.user_value: Proves --dangerously-skip-permissions is safe inside a locked-down container.
acceptance_criteria: Container spec generator enforces every field

capabilities.title: Restricted Docker network with HTTP allowlist proxy
capabilities.description: Docker network claude-restricted with tinyproxy/Squid sidecar allowing only api.anthropic.com, registry.npmjs.org, GitHub IP ranges. Containers use HTTP_PROXY env var.
capabilities.user_value: Prevents exfiltration, crypto mining, C2.

capabilities.title: ContainerPool class mirroring PtyPool interface
capabilities.description: ContainerPool with same external interface as PtyPool (allocate, deallocate, list, stats). Tracks container-id → job-id, enforces max concurrency, reports CPU/memory/PID stats.
capabilities.user_value: Backend swap is transparent to hub-side scheduling.

capabilities.title: Pre-pull Claude Code image on agent startup
capabilities.description: Agent pulls ghcr.io/anthropics/claude-code:latest on startup; weekly refresh. Startup health check fails on pull failure.
capabilities.user_value: Sub-second container startup (no 30s pull penalty).
acceptance_criteria: Health check fails on pull failure

capabilities.title: Container lifecycle and exit handling with commit+push
capabilities.description: Agent calls container.wait(); on exit, reports container:exited with code, runs git add/commit/push in worktree, removes container, optionally creates PR, removes worktree.
capabilities.user_value: Clean shutdown with automatic code commit and resource cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Cleanup in both success and failure paths

capabilities.title: Container stdout/stderr streaming via WebSocket
capabilities.description: Agent.attach + demuxStream. Chunks sent as { type: 'container:stdout'|'container:stderr', containerId, data }. Hub forwards to subscribed dashboard clients.
capabilities.user_value: Real-time container output without polling.

capabilities.title: Container resource stats reporting
capabilities.description: Agent queries Docker stats API per running container every 5-10s, reports { cpuPercent, memoryMB, pids } via container:stats. Hub stores and serves via metrics query endpoint.
capabilities.user_value: Per-job resource tracking for observability and tuning.

capabilities.title: Execution mode selection (docker | pty) with fallback
capabilities.description: Config option executionMode per machine or per job. Docker mode for autonomous/queued; pty for interactive. Auto-fallback to pty if Docker unavailable with warning.
capabilities.user_value: Gradual migration path and mixed workloads.

capabilities.title: Async setup commands in temporary container
capabilities.description: Before main Claude container, agent runs setup_commands in a temp container mounting the same worktree (extended timeout). On failure, reports container:error and aborts main container.
capabilities.user_value: Dependencies/build artifacts ready before Claude starts; no permission dialogs inside the main container.
acceptance_criteria: Setup container created with same worktree

capabilities.title: Auto-accept permissions model (--dangerously-skip + sandbox)
capabilities.description: In docker mode, Claude Code invoked with --dangerously-skip-permissions; container boundary replaces permission system. Requires CAP-081 (security baseline) and CAP-082 (restricted network) to be ON.
capabilities.user_value: Fully autonomous sessions without interactive permission dialogs.
acceptance_criteria: Flag only applied inside containers meeting baseline

capabilities.title: Worktree and container retention cleanup
capabilities.description: Background job removes worktrees and stopped containers older than retention period (default 7 days) while respecting in-flight jobs. Logs removals.
capabilities.user_value: Bounded disk usage without manual cleanup.

capabilities.title: Container orchestration WebSocket protocol schema
capabilities.description: Zod schemas and TS types for container:create/stop/remove/exec (hub→agent) and container:created/started/stdout/stderr/exited/error/stats (agent→hub). Version-negotiated.
capabilities.user_value: Strongly typed protocol for container orchestration.

capabilities.title: Single-container Hub serves Dashboard static files
capabilities.description: Hub uses @fastify/static to serve Dashboard SPA. Dashboard built with nuxt generate, copied into Hub image. setNotFoundHandler routes non-API paths to index.html. Relative API/WS paths.
capabilities.user_value: Minimal deployment: one container, one health check, one log stream.

capabilities.title: Multi-stage Dockerfile with turbo prune for combined Hub+Dashboard
capabilities.description: 6-stage Dockerfile (base, pruner, hub-deps, hub-builder, dashboard-builder, prod-deps, runtime). turbo prune @chq/hub --docker, turbo prune @chq/dashboard --docker --out-dir out-dashboard. BuildKit cache mount for pnpm store.
capabilities.user_value: Fast reproducible builds with optimal layer caching.
acceptance_criteria: turbo prune used for both packages
Final image runs with docker build

capabilities.title: Node 22 bookworm-slim base image (glibc)
capabilities.description: Base image node:22-bookworm-slim. hub-deps and prod-deps stages install python3, make, g++ for better-sqlite3 native compilation. Never Alpine (musl fcntl64 issues).
capabilities.user_value: Reliable SQLite compilation and no libc symbol failures.
acceptance_criteria: Build tools present in deps stages

capabilities.title: Docker Compose with Tailscale sidecar
capabilities.description: compose with tailscale service (image tailscale/tailscale:latest) and hub service (network_mode: service:tailscale). TS_AUTHKEY, TS_STATE_DIR, TS_SERVE_CONFIG, TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-tags=tag:claudehq. ts-state named volume.
capabilities.user_value: Hub auto-joins tailnet with stable hostname without host-level Tailscale install.

capabilities.title: Tailscale Serve HTTPS config
capabilities.description: deploy/ts-config/serve.json: TCP 443 HTTPS, Web handler at ${TS_CERT_DOMAIN}:443 → http://127.0.0.1:7700. Auto TLS via Tailscale managed certs.
capabilities.user_value: Secure HTTPS access with zero cert management.

capabilities.title: Simplified docker-compose for host Tailscale
capabilities.description: docker-compose.simple.yml variant: hub only, ports: ['7700:7700'], no Tailscale sidecar. For hosts already running Tailscale.
capabilities.user_value: Fewer moving parts for operators with host-level Tailscale.

capabilities.title: Docker secrets for API keys and GitHub private key
capabilities.description: Compose secrets: section with anthropic_api_key and github_app_private_key. Hub reads from /run/secrets/*. .gitignore excludes secrets/, .env, data/. TS_AUTHKEY via .env.
capabilities.user_value: Secrets never appear in env vars or compose file; git-safe.

capabilities.title: SQLite backup strategy (cron .backup + Litestream)
capabilities.description: Host cron runs docker exec hub sqlite3 ... '.backup <file>' with timestamp. Optional Litestream sidecar for continuous S3/B2/SFTP replication.
capabilities.user_value: Reliable recovery from container failure.

capabilities.title: Agent Dockerfile for remote deployment
capabilities.description: Dockerfile.agent (separate from hub compose) with same multi-stage pattern. Runtime stage includes procps for node-pty process management.
capabilities.user_value: Consistent lean agent images for containerized deployments (bare-metal systemd remains primary).
acceptance_criteria: Agent container connects to hub WS

capabilities.title: .dockerignore for monorepo build context
capabilities.description: .dockerignore excludes node_modules/, .nuxt/, .output/, dist/, .turbo/, .git/, .github/, .vscode/, .claude/, docs/, *.md, .env*, secrets/, data/.
capabilities.user_value: Smaller build context; no accidental secret/data inclusion.
acceptance_criteria: .dockerignore in repo root with all patterns

capabilities.title: Hub memory limit via NODE_OPTIONS
capabilities.description: NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=384 in hub service. Node.js doesn't auto-detect Docker memory limits.
capabilities.user_value: Predictable memory bounds, no OOM kills.

capabilities.title: Competitive landscape doc (claudeHQ vs alternatives)
capabilities.description: docs/market/ summary comparing claudeHQ to OpenClaw, runCLAUDErun, manual Claude Code, standalone Claude. Key differentiator: distributed orchestration + live terminal + replay.
capabilities.user_value: Clear positioning for product decisions and communication.

dedup_log.reason: github-docker-costs-rivet research gives the fuller manifest flow + credential storage pattern; workforce version collapsed into those capabilities

dedup_log.merged: workforce LOCAL-009 (cost tracking) + remote-console LOCAL-013 (session timeout/cost)
dedup_log.reason: split into capture / formula / per-query budget / period thresholds / enforcement
split umbrella abstraction into per-backend strategies
persistence, playback UI, and retention are separate deliverables

dedup_log.merged: docs-audit LOCAL-002 (HTTP hooks matrix fix) + LOCAL-005 (PermissionRequest/TaskCompleted) + approval LOCAL-013/014 (hook receivers)
dedup_log.reason: single implementation with the corrected HTTP-vs-WS matrix
agent daemon core already covers the lifecycle channel

dedup_log.merged: gh-docker LOCAL-016 (typed permission replies) ~ approval LOCAL-008 (three-way decision)
dedup_log.reason: related but distinct: three-way decision is approve/edit/reject; once/always/reject is session-scoped caching. Kept both

dedup_log.merged: container-orch LOCAL-004 (git worktree from container side)
dedup_log.reason: worktree lifecycle consolidated into a single capability shared by both backends
both part of the hub core server capability
